name: E-mail protection
summary: Suricata will detect most attempt to abuse e-mail protocols such as IMAP, POP or SMTP

name: File transfert protection
summary: Suricata will detect most attempt to abuse file transfert protocols, such as FTP, TFTP

name: Exploit protection
summary: Suricata will detect known bug exploitation on various platforms

name: Crypto currency miner detection
summary: Suricata will detect attempts to mine crypto currency, which can be normal or the result of a malware

name: Scan detection
summary: Suricata try to detect active network scans

name: Ping protection
summary: Suricata will detect wrong usage of the ICMP protocol

name: Microsoft ActiveX protection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks against Microsoft ActiveX controls and exploits targeting vulnerabilities in ActiveX controls

name: Ad and spyware detection
summary: Suricata will try to identify software that is used for ad tracking or other types of spyware related activity

name: Attack Response detection
summary: Suricata try to detect results of a succesfull attack

name: Bot Command and Control rules
summary: Suricata will detect a known botnet

name: Chat traffic protection
summary: Suricata will identify traffic related to numerous chat clients such as Internet Relay Chat (IRC).

name: CIArmy ip list detection
summary: Suricata will detect if a known compromised machine tries to communicate

name: DNS traffic protection
summary: Suricata will identify attacks and vulnerabilities regarding Domain Name Service (DNS). This category is also used for rules related to abuse of DNS such as tunneling

name: DOS tentatives detection
summary: Suricata will detect Denial of Service (DoS) attempts. These rules are intended to catch inbound DoS activity, and provide indication of outbound DoS activity.

name: Unwanted traffic detection
summary:
Suricata will identify unappropriate traffic, such as gaming or pornography. 
 Warning : This category can have a significant performance impact and high rate of false positives

name: Threat hunting
summary: Suricata will provide indicators that when matched with other signatures can be very useful for threat hunting in an environment. These rules can provide false positives on legitimate traffic and inhibit performance. They are only recommended for use when actively researching potential threats in the environment.

name: Info detection
summary: Suricata will help help provide audit level events that are useful for correlation and identifying interesting activity which may not be inherently malicious but is often observed in malware and other threats, for example downloading an Executable over HTTP by IP address rather than domain name.

name: Malware detection
summary: Suricata will detect malicious software. Rules in this category detect activity related to malicious software that is detected on the network including malware in transit, active malware, malware infections, malware attacks, and updating of malware.

name: Mobile malware detection
summary: Suricata will detect malwares associated with mobile operating systems, such as Android

name: Netbios traffic protection
summary: Suricata will detect traffic related to attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding NetBIOS. This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious NetBIOS activity for logging purposes

name: Peer to Peer (P2P) traffic detection
summary: Suricata will detect P2P usage and attacks. Identified P2P traffic includes torrents, edonkey, Bittorrent, Gnutella and Limewire among others.

name: Phishing detection
summary: Suricata will detect credential phishing activity. This includes landing pages exhibiting credential phishing as well as successful submission of credentials into credential phishing sites

name: Policy violation detection
summary: Suricata will detect violations to an organization’s policy. This can include protocols prone to abuse, and other application-level transactions which may be of interest.

name: RPC abuse detection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding Remote Procedure Call (RPC). This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious RPC activity for logging purposes.

name: SCADA abuse detection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA). This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious SCADA activity for logging purposes.

name: Shellcode detection
summary: Suricata will detect remote shellcode. Remote shellcode is used when an attacker wants to target a vulnerable process running on another machine on a local network or intranet

name: SNMP abuse detection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP). This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious SNMP activity for logging purposes

name: SQL abuse detection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding Structured Query Language (SQL). This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious SQL activity for logging purposes

name: Telnet abuse detection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks, exploits, and vulnerabilities regarding TELNET. This category also includes rules that detect non-malicious TELNET activity for logging purposes.

name: TOR detection
summary: Suricata will detect TOR traffic to and from exit nodes based on adresses

name: User agent protection
summary: Suricata will detect suspicious and anomalous user agents.

name: User agent protection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks and vulnerabilities regarding Voice over IP (VOIP) including SIP, H.323 and RTP among others.

name: Web Client protection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks and vulnerabilities regarding web clients such as web browsers as well as client side applications like CURL, WGET and others.

name: Web Server protection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks against web server infrastructure such as APACHE, TOMCAT, NGINX, Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) and other web server software

name: Web Applications protection
summary: Suricata will detect attacks and vulnerabilities in specific web applications

name: Worms detection
summary: Suricata will detect malicious activity that automatically attempts to spread across the internet or within a network by exploiting a vulnerability are classified as the WORM category. While the actual exploit itself will typically be identified in the Exploit or given protocol category, an additional entry in this category may be made if the actual malware engaging in worm-like propagation can be identified as well.

name: Miscellaneous
summary: This category is for rules that does not fit in other category
